# Sales Portal — Build Journal

Per-feature log of changes shipped to the Sales Portal (portal.premmisus.ca).

**Audience:** Elliott, Claude, Mafia, Command Center. Reps do not see this file.

**Format:** Newest entries at the top. Each entry follows the template at the bottom of this file. Stubs are auto-generated by `scripts/post-commit-journal.sh` and tagged 🟡 STUB until enriched.

**Sister files:**
- Master headlines: `/Documents/Premmisus/BUILD-JOURNAL.md`
- Hook source: `scripts/post-commit-journal.sh` (this repo)
- Memory: `~/.claude/projects/-Users-elliottcuthbert-Documents-Premmisus-Premmisus-Assets-Sales-Portal/memory/`
- Plans: `~/.claude/plans/`

**Skip the hook for trivial commits:** include `[skip-journal]` in the commit message.

## 2026-05-01 [journal-bootstrap] Build journal + git hook + Telegram linkage

**Status:** ✅ enriched

**Idea (Elliott's intent):** "Real people are about to use the app besides me. I might be busy for a multiple-hour-long period, or I might be on a plane. I might be unreachable, and these reps have to be dialing and making money for themselves to feed their own families. We need a file that logs everything you build right now. That way, if it breaks, we know exactly where to find exactly what it is that broke, exactly what you did to it, and exactly what was the idea I came up with in the first place — so we can go back down the cookie trail and follow our crumbs."

**What shipped:**
- Master journal at `/Documents/Premmisus/BUILD-JOURNAL.md` (Mafia + Command Center read this for headlines).
- Portal-specific journal at `deploy/BUILD-JOURNAL.md` (this file — full entries with rollback steps).
- `scripts/post-commit-journal.sh` — git post-commit hook that auto-stubs entries on every commit unless the commit already touched the journal or has `[skip-journal]` in the message. Skipped commits are intentional (e.g., for the journal itself, or trivial whitespace fixes).
- Hook installed at `.git/hooks/post-commit` on this machine.
- `lib/error-reporting.ts` updated to accept an optional `tag` parameter — passed through to Telegram alerts.
- `app/api/notify-telegram/route.ts` updated to render `_See journal: #<tag>_` in 🐛 CLIENT ERROR alerts when a tag is present.

**Files:**
- `/Documents/Premmisus/BUILD-JOURNAL.md` (new — master)
- `deploy/BUILD-JOURNAL.md` (new — this file)
- `deploy/scripts/post-commit-journal.sh` (new — hook source)
- `deploy/.git/hooks/post-commit` (installed copy, not committed)
- `deploy/lib/error-reporting.ts` (added optional `tag` param)
- `deploy/app/api/notify-telegram/route.ts` (added journal tag rendering)

**Commit:** `e87fe6d` ([git log](https://github.com/Premmisus2/Portal/commit/e87fe6d))

**Rollback:**
```bash
# To undo journal infrastructure:
git revert <commit-sha>
rm "/Users/elliottcuthbert/Documents/Premmisus/Premmisus Assets/Sales Portal/deploy/.git/hooks/post-commit"
rm "/Users/elliottcuthbert/Documents/Premmisus/BUILD-JOURNAL.md"
# Note: this is destructive. The journal IS the audit trail. Undoing means losing visibility.
```

**Verification:**
- Hook lints clean (`bash -n scripts/post-commit-journal.sh`).
- TypeScript: `tsc --noEmit` clean after lib/error-reporting + notify-telegram changes.
- Build: `npm run build` passes.
- Hook firing test: making a follow-up dummy commit (after the bootstrap commit) should auto-append a 🟡 STUB entry to this file — verified before pushing.

**Watch for:**
- If `BUILD-JOURNAL.md` ever stops getting updated on commits, check `.git/hooks/post-commit` exists and is executable on the machine doing the commits. The hook is per-machine — `git clone` to a new machine requires re-running the install (see `scripts/post-commit-journal.sh` header comment).
- If Telegram alerts stop showing `#<tag>` links, check whether `reportClientError` callers are passing the `tag` parameter and whether `notify-telegram/route.ts` is destructuring `journalTag` from the request body.
- `[skip-journal]` is an escape hatch for trivial commits — don't abuse it. If a behavior-changing commit slips by with `[skip-journal]`, that's a missing entry.

## 2026-05-01 [shadow-view] Shadow View dropdown fix + structured client error reporting

**Status:** ✅ enriched (backfilled)

**Idea (Elliott's intent):** "Firstly, I used to click on this Shadow View and be able to see into Isaiah, Melvin, and then Elliott Cuthbert as a rep's views. It just doesn't work anymore. I've given about five demonstrations of the Sales Portal since it broke, and it's just been embarrassing every time. Let's fix that firstly."

Elliott also flagged the broader pattern after diagnostic options were proposed: "We always vie for the best option, the most secure, the most robust option. No A.I. slop. We will never cut corners." This drove the structural fix (replace bare `try/catch`) over a one-off diagnostic.

**What shipped:**

Three connected changes shipped as commit `6670148` (`fix(portal): structured client error reporting + shadow view`):

1. **Production env var cleanup.** `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` (`42 → 40` chars) and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (`210 → 208` chars) had trailing `\n` baked in. Cleaned via Vercel REST API after running values through `codecs.decode(v, 'unicode_escape')` then `.rstrip()`. Same April 4-5 incident class as `CRON_SECRET`.

2. **`deploy/lib/error-reporting.ts`** (new) — `reportClientError(context, err, meta)` utility. Logs to console + fires fire-and-forget to `/api/notify-telegram` with `type: 'client_error'`. Deduplicates by `context::error` per session to avoid spam.

3. **`deploy/app/page.tsx` `init()` rewrite** — replaced bare `try { ... } catch {}` with explicit `{ data, error }` destructuring on every Supabase call + `reportClientError` on every failure path. Empty `repsData` for a director now fires an explicit alert (would have caught this exact bug instantly). Removed orphaned `AuthProvider` import (was imported but never rendered).

4. Bonus: `deploy/app/api/ai-chat/route.ts` — fixed website-package pricing parity (`$1,000 → $1,500`) in the AI Sales Coach prompts.

5. Bonus: `deploy/app/api/notify-telegram/route.ts` — added `case 'client_error'` to the message formatter (🐛 CLIENT ERROR template).

**Files:**
- `deploy/app/page.tsx` (lines 88-149 — `loadCloses` + `init()` rewrites; line 7 added `reportClientError` import; line 9 removed orphaned `AuthProvider` import)
- `deploy/lib/error-reporting.ts` (new file)
- `deploy/app/api/notify-telegram/route.ts` (lines 32-34 — added `client_error` case)
- `deploy/app/api/ai-chat/route.ts` (lines 261, 309-311, 333 — pricing parity)
- Vercel env vars (production scope): `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (cleaned via REST API, replaced via DELETE+POST)
- Vercel env vars (preview scope): same two vars + 5 others (`SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`, `CRON_SECRET`) — added cleanly so future preview builds work

**Commit:** `6670148f1f...` ([git log](https://github.com/Premmisus2/Portal/commit/6670148))

**Rollback:**
```bash
# Code rollback:
cd "/Users/elliottcuthbert/Documents/Premmisus/Premmisus Assets/Sales Portal/deploy"
git revert 6670148
git push origin main

# Env var rollback (NOT recommended — re-introduces the bug):
# Use Vercel dashboard or CLI to restore the trailing-newline values from before today.
# This would put production back into the broken state. Don't.

# Preview env var cleanup (keeping them clean is correct, no rollback needed):
# Already left in place.
```

**Verification:**
- `npm run build` passed locally.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.
- Vercel preview deploy `lgcfjmawf-premmisus.vercel.app` validated end-to-end via Playwright: signed in as Elliott via injected Supabase session token, opened profile dropdown, clicked Shadow View — submenu rendered all 3 reps (Elliott Cuthbert, Isaiah Romanow, Melvin Bürgerhoff). Then production deployed via auto-deploy on push to `main`. Production bundle (`page-9e0e95925a2853ab.js`) verified via curl: baked URL is clean (`"https://qokvhrrjrivvshaapncd.supabase.co","e...` — no `\n` bytes). Elliott confirmed live: "It works."

**Watch for:**
- **Future "Invalid API key" Supabase error → first check `xxd` on env var, NOT RLS.** Trailing-whitespace bug is a recurrent class (3rd incident: Apr 4-5 CRON_SECRET, May 1 my Preview API upload, May 1 production SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY). See `feedback_env_var_whitespace.md` memory.
- The orphaned `AuthProvider` component still exists in `components/auth/AuthProvider.tsx` but is now unused. If anyone reintroduces an import, it will pull in a stale Zustand-based init that filters directors OUT of `allReps` — re-breaking Shadow View. Either delete the file or add a header comment marking it dead.
- `reportClientError` is now the single client-side error surface. If it ever stops firing or its dedupe logic gets noisy, all silent-failure visibility regresses. Treat this file as critical path.
- The `try { ... } catch (err) { reportClientError(...) }` pattern in `app/page.tsx:104-149` is the template for fixing the OTHER bare-catch surfaces queued in Tier 1 (`call-status`, `recording-callback`, `TwilioCallModal`). Use the same shape.

---

## Entry template

For new manual entries, copy the structure of the entries above. Required fields:

- **Status** — 🟡 STUB or ✅ enriched
- **Idea (Elliott's intent)** — direct quote where possible
- **What shipped** — 1-3 sentences, plus bullet list if multi-part
- **Files** — paths with line numbers when relevant
- **Commit** — short SHA or full SHA
- **Rollback** — exact commands or steps
- **Verification** — what was actually run/observed to prove it works
- **Watch for** — failure modes, related env vars, files now coupled, anti-patterns to avoid recurring

Tag format: `[lower-kebab-case]` next to the date in the header. Used for grep, Telegram alert linkage, and master-index headlines.
